rationale: The pair shows strong conceptual and methodological overlap: both papers model eye-tracking attention in relation to utility and choice, and the cited paper is a prior attention–choice process study (goal-driven fixations, utility-linked attention) in conjoint decisions. Given the citing paper explicitly claims to extend prior attention-and-choice work with trajectory modeling and prediction, this citation is plausibly foundational/method-relevant rather than an obvious incidental mention.
